user_message: This just came in from Thomas Okafor at Azure Ridge Partners. Can you generate the watchlist?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 10:47 EST
Subject: consumption trend - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 17:53 EST
Subject: Follow-up: consumption trend Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Azure Ridge Partners
Date: 2024-03-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Priya (Azure Ridge Partners)

[00:02:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:12:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:24:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:29:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:33:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
